justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with explicit math (200+150+800=1150ms > 500ms), (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — explains that the enrichment service continues making calls nobody will read, wasting capacity and cascading under load, (3) Outbound > inbound timeout relationship explicitly called out ('CRITICALLY MISCONFIGURED', '6x'), (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, reducing outbound timeouts to fit inbound budget, making InventoryService async/optional, partial enrichment, context cancellation propagation, (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough timeout budget analysis — not only computes the 1150ms sum but also notes that InventoryService p99 alone (800ms) exceeds the 500ms budget, meaning parallelization alone is insufficient without renegotiating the inbound timeout
Correctly identifies the wasted-work cascade: upstream abandons at 500ms but downstream calls continue for up to 1000ms each, with clear resource exhaustion implications

Provides more than the minimum two remediation options: parallelization, timeout adjustment, async/optional InventoryService, context cancellation propagation, partial enrichment with graceful degradation

Goes beyond the rubric by identifying additional production risks: missing circuit breakers, thread pool exhaustion from 3000ms worst-case hold time, health check coupling to flaky downstream services
Escalation routing to MJ is appropriate — correctly frames the timeout budget as an architectural decision rather than a config fix
Confidence calibration is reasonable at 60% — acknowledges uncertainty about business requirements for partial enrichment while being fully confident in the mathematical timeout analysis
